question_text: A petrol car engine converts chemical energy from fuel into kinetic energy (movement) with an efficiency of 25%. The remaining 75% becomes heat energy. If the car uses 1,200 J of chemical energy to travel 50 metres, calculate how much heat energy is wasted and explain whether this violates conservation of energy.
correct_answer: 900 J of heat is wasted. This does not violate conservation of energy because the 900 J is still energy—it has been transformed into heat (which goes into the engine block and exhaust) rather than being destroyed. Total energy (300 J kinetic + 900 J heat) = 1,200 J input.
explanation: Students must calculate the wasted energy (75% of 1,200 = 900 J) and then explain that energy is still conserved even when transformed into unwanted heat. This reinforces the difference between energy being 'wasted' (used inefficiently) and energy being 'destroyed' (impossible).
